params.name: Hachiko Statue
params.description:
On Sept 6, 1934 AD, the statue of loyal Hachiko was officially erected at the train station to commemorate him.
This statue was erected to commemorate the loyal dog, Hachiko. His owner died of a heart attack at work, but Hachiko still awaited his return in front of the train station. After 9 years of waiting, in 1932, the Hachiko's story was published by a newspaper, and soon the story spread throughout Japan. Touched by the dog's loyalty. Eventually, people erected a statue of Hachiko in fro …
